title: Annual Financial Report (Option 1 REPs)
desc: Annual financial report for Option 1 REPs including balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement. Filed with PUCT via the Interchange Filing System. Demonstrates ongoing financial integrity requirements for REP certification.

title: Semi-Annual Compliance Report (Option 1 REPs)
desc: Semi-annual compliance report filed approximately 6 months after the annual financial report. Covers ongoing compliance status for Option 1 REPs during the interim period.

title: Comprehensive Annual Report (Customer Data)
desc: Annual report on customer data metrics including number of customers served by rate class, complaints received, disconnections, and other PUCT-required data points. Filed by March 15 each year.

title: REC Compliance Report
desc: Renewable Energy Certificate (REC) compliance report demonstrating REC obligations have been met for the prior compliance year. Coordinate with ERCOT REC program data. Filed with PUCT by March 15.

title: Letter of Credit Maintenance
desc: Continuous maintenance of the required letter of credit or surety bond with PUCT. Must ensure LOC/bond remains current, adequate in amount, and never lapses. Any upcoming expiration triggers the OO-04 90-day notice requirement.

title: Electricity Facts Label (EFL) Filing
desc: EFL must be filed with PUCT prior to offering any new electricity product to customers. Also required when existing product terms change materially. Filed via PUCT Interchange Filing System before product launch.

title: Terms of Service (ToS) Filing
desc: Terms of Service document must be filed with PUCT for each new product offering or when material changes occur. Must be provided to customers prior to or at the time of enrollment.

title: Your Rights as a Customer (YRAC) Filing
desc: YRAC document must be provided to all residential and small commercial customers. Updated when PUCT rules change or when new product offerings are launched. Keep current version on file.

title: Customer Complaint Response (PUCT)
desc: Formal complaints forwarded by PUCT must be responded to within 15 days. Maintain a log of all PUCT-forwarded complaints, response dates, and resolution outcomes.

title: Material Change Amendment
desc: Any material change to REP certification information must be reported to PUCT within 10 working days. This includes ownership changes, key personnel changes, financial changes, and changes to the LOC/bond.

title: Record Retention Compliance
desc: Maintain all customer records, contracts, complaints, billing records, and regulatory correspondence for a minimum of 2 years as required by PUCT rules. Records must be accessible for audit or regulatory review.

title: TX Secretary of State — Active Registration
desc: Maintain active foreign or domestic registration with Texas SOS. Keep registered agent information current. Annual franchise tax and PIR due May 15 (see SOS-02 and SOS-06). Ensure no lapse in good standing.

title: Customer Acquisition / Transfer Notice
desc: Notice requirements when acquiring or transferring a significant block of customer accounts. Timing and form depend on transaction specifics. Coordinate with PUCT staff before any major customer portfolio transaction.

title: Prepaid Service Notice (if applicable)
desc: If Base Power offers any prepaid electricity products, specific disclosure and customer notice requirements apply under PUCT Rule 25.498. Review applicability if prepaid products are introduced.

title: Retail Market Performance Measure Report (§25.88)
desc: NEW rule effective April 2, 2026. Quarterly Retail Market Performance Measures report filed with PUCT no later than 45 days after the end of each quarterly period (Jan 1, Apr 1, Jul 1, Oct 1). Begins with Q2 2026 — first filing due August 14, 2026. Report covers: (1) Competitive market indicators; (2) Technical market mechanics; (3) Field performance statistics. Each submission must include analysis of any measure not meeting expected performance levels, plus a signed and notarized affidavit by an executive officer per §25.107. Supporting documents must be retained for 24 months. PUCT may impose administrative penalties under §22.246 for failure to timely file.
dln: 45 days after quarter end — first due Aug 14, 2026 (Q2)
instr:
1. Obtain PUCT-prescribed filing forms when published.
2. Collect data for all three performance measure categories each quarter.
3. For any measure below expected performance level, prepare written analysis of the change vs. prior quarter and contributing circumstances.
4. Have an executive officer sign and notarize the report attestation affidavit per §25.107.
5. File electronically via PUCT Interchange Filing System in native file format.
6. Retain all supporting records and documentation for 24 months after each report date.

title: Annual Officer Certification
desc: Annual officer certification required for all ERCOT Market Participants. Must be signed by an authorized officer of the company certifying compliance with ERCOT Nodal Protocols. Due April 30 each year.

title: Audited Financial Statements & Risk Certification
desc: Annual submission of CPA-audited financial statements and risk certification to ERCOT by April 30. Financial statements must be audited by a qualified independent CPA firm. Risk certification covers trading and credit risk management.

title: Credit Limit / TPE Compliance
desc: Continuous monitoring and maintenance of credit exposure against ERCOT Transmission-related, Portfolio, and Exposure (TPE) limits. Post additional collateral if exposure approaches limits. Monitor daily.

title: EFL Renewable Content Update
desc: Annual update to EFL renewable content information as of July 1. Reflects the previous calendar year's renewable energy mix and REC retirement data. Must coordinate with PUCT EFL filing (TX-06) to ensure consistency.

title: Responsive Device Program Data (Quarterly)
desc: Quarterly submission of responsive device program data to ERCOT. Due 45 days after quarter end: Q1→May 15, Q2→Aug 14, Q3→Nov 14, Q4→Feb 14. Required for demand response program participation tracking.

title: Annual DR Survey Confirmation
desc: Two-part annual filing: (1) Confirm DR program participation by Aug 15; (2) Submit full list of participating ESI IDs by Oct 15. Required for all market participants enrolled in ERCOT demand response programs.

title: Contact Information Updates
desc: Any changes to key contact information (emergency contacts, billing contacts, authorized representatives) must be reported to ERCOT within 5 business days of the change via the ERCOT registration portal.

title: QSE Relationship Maintenance
desc: Maintain active and current QSE (Qualified Scheduling Entity) agreement. Ensure QSE is correctly scheduling load, the QSE relationship does not lapse, and QSE has current authorization to act on behalf of Base Power.

title: ADER Telemetry (if participating)
desc: If Base Power participates in the ERCOT Aggregated Distributed Energy Resource (ADER) Phase 3 pilot, real-time telemetry data must be provided at 2-second intervals. Requires reliable communication infrastructure.

title: Settlement Metering Compliance
desc: Ensure all settlement metering (interval data, meter reading, and reporting) complies with ERCOT Section 10 requirements. Monitor data quality, verify readings against expectations, and respond to settlement disputes promptly.

title: Emergency Operations Plan (EOP) — Annual Update
desc: Annual update and resubmission of the Emergency Operations Plan by March 15. Dual filing required: (1) unredacted version to ERCOT; (2) redacted version to PUCT via Interchange Filing System. Must reflect current personnel, procedures, and operational capabilities.

title: EOP Annual Emergency Drill
desc: Annual emergency operations drill must be conducted and documented before December 31. PUCT must be given 30 days advance written notice before the drill date. Document all results, gaps identified, and corrective actions taken.
dln: Before Dec 31; 30-day PUCT advance notice required

title: EOP Initial Filing (New REP Certification)
desc: One-time initial EOP filing due within 10 days of receiving PUCT REP certification. Track for any new subsidiary or affiliate REP certifications. This item is likely already complete for Base Power's existing certification.

title: Rate Change Customer Notice
dln: 14 days advance written notice to customers
desc: When changing rates or pricing for existing fixed-price customers, must provide at least 14 days advance written notice. Notice must include the new rate, effective date, and customer's rights to switch providers.

title: Contract Expiration Notice
desc: Must notify customers at least 30 days before their fixed-price contract expires. Notice must clearly describe renewal options, default product terms, and the customer's right to shop for a new provider.

title: Cessation of Operations Notice
desc: If Base Power decides to cease operations as a REP in Texas, PUCT must be notified immediately upon making that decision. PUCT will coordinate the customer transfer process and wind-down procedures.

title: Letter of Credit Expiration Notice
desc: Must notify PUCT at least 90 days before the Letter of Credit or surety bond expires to allow sufficient time for renewal. Failure to maintain a current LOC can result in suspension of REP certification.

title: Notice of Change of Affiliates (ERCOT)
desc: Must notify ERCOT of any material changes in affiliate relationships on a timely basis. Affiliate changes may trigger updated Standards of Conduct obligations or require amendments to existing market participant agreements.

title: LSIPA Attestation / Section 23 Form S
desc: Load Serving Integrated Planning Agreement (LSIPA) attestation and Section 23 Form S submissions per ERCOT-published deadlines. Monitor ERCOT communications for specific submission dates each year.

title: Forced Outage Reporting (ERCOT)
desc: Any forced outage affecting ERCOT settlement obligations must be reported through the ERCOT portal within 60 minutes of occurrence. Include outage details, affected ESI IDs, cause, and estimated restoration time.

title: Part 451 Continuing Compliance Report
desc: Annual continuing compliance report filed with Illinois Commerce Commission by April 30. Demonstrates ongoing compliance with ARES certification requirements including financial, operational, and marketing standards.

title: Rates Charged to Residential Customers
desc: Annual report of rates charged to Illinois residential customers filed with ICC by April 30. Includes required pricing data and rate comparison information under ARES obligations.

title: Financial Reporting (ICC)
desc: Annual financial reporting to ICC. Credit-rated entities file ratings by April 30; audited financial statements due within 120 days of fiscal year end. Confirm current requirements with ICC staff each year.

title: RPS Compliance Report (ICC)
desc: Annual Renewable Portfolio Standard (RPS) compliance report filed with ICC by September 1. Must demonstrate renewable energy procurement meets the required Illinois RPS percentage for the compliance year.

title: Environmental Disclosure Report (Quarterly)
desc: Quarterly environmental disclosure report filed with ICC. Covers fuel mix and emissions data for electricity supplied. Q1: Apr 30 | Q2: Jul 31 | Q3: Oct 31 | Q4: Jan 31.
dln: Q1: Apr 30 | Q2: Jul 31 | Q3: Oct 31 | Q4: Jan 31

title: Surety Bond Maintenance (ICC)
desc: Continuous maintenance of required surety bond or letter of credit with ICC. Monitor bond renewal dates, ensure adequate coverage levels, and never allow a lapse. Bond failure can result in ARES certification suspension.

title: Informal Complaint Response (ICC)
desc: Informal complaints forwarded by ICC must be responded to within 14 days. Maintain a complaint log. Formal complaints may require hearing participation and more extensive responses.

title: Telemarketing Call Recording Retention
desc: All telemarketing sales calls in Illinois must be recorded and retained per ICC requirements. Maintain recording system compliance, retention schedule, and ensure recordings are available to ICC upon request.

title: Marketing Materials Compliance
desc: All Illinois marketing materials must comply with ICC disclosure requirements before use. Include required disclosures on pricing methodology, contract terms, cancellation rights, and customer protections.

title: UDS / Sales Contract Compliance
desc: All Uniform Disclosure Statements (UDS) and sales contracts must comply with ICC requirements. Ensure UDS is provided to customers at the time of sale with all required disclosures, cancellation rights, and terms.

title: Customer Account Transfer Report (ICC)
desc: Report any customer account transfers (acquisitions or divestitures) to ICC within 30 days of the transaction closing. Required when acquiring or divesting a significant block of Illinois customer accounts.

title: ARES Filing Information Update
desc: Must update ARES certification information with ICC within 10 business days of any material change to company information, ownership, key personnel, or operational details.

title: Credit Agreement Change Notice
desc: Must provide ICC at least 15 days advance notice before any change to the credit agreement, surety bond, or other financial assurance mechanism supporting ARES certification.

title: Designated Agent Report — Annual Filing
desc: Annual Designated Agent (DA) report filed with ICC between January 1 and January 31 each year. Confirms or updates the identity and contact information of the company's designated agent on file with the Commission. Required for all ARES. Cover letter and verification page with original signatures required. Must match company name exactly as it appears on the ICC Public Utility Database.
instr:
1. Confirm current DA information on file with ICC via the entity profile page.
2. If DA is unchanged, file confirmation of existing DA.
3. If DA has changed, use the change-based report (IL-15) instead.
4. Include cover letter on company letterhead with company full name (including any DBAs) and citation to 83 Ill. Adm. Code 215.10(a).
5. Include verification page with original signatures.
6. File by mail to: Chief Clerk's Office, Illinois Commerce Commission, 527 E. Capitol Ave., Springfield, IL 62701 — or electronically if authorized.

title: Designated Agent Report — Change Filing
dln: 15 days after any change to the Designated Agent
desc: Must file an updated Designated Agent report with ICC within 15 days of any change to the company's Designated Agent (DA). The DA is the individual authorized to receive official ICC communications on behalf of the company. Cover letter and verification page with original signatures required.
instr:
1. When a DA change occurs, promptly prepare the updated Designated Agent Report.
2. Include cover letter on company letterhead citing 83 Ill. Adm. Code 215.50.
3. Include verification page with original signatures.
4. File within 15 days of the change.

title: Call Center Information Report
desc: Annual report of call center information filed with ICC by March 1. Reports details on the ARES customer call center including location, hours of operation, staffing, and performance metrics. Required for all ARES serving Illinois retail customers.
instr:
1. Collect call center information: location(s), hours of operation, number of customer service representatives, average wait times, call volume data.
2. Prepare report in ICC-required format.
3. Include cover letter citing 83 Ill. Adm. Code 410.45(c) and 220 ILCS 5/16-123.
4. File by March 1 with ICC Chief Clerk's Office.

title: Conduct, Training and Compliance of RES Agents Certification
desc: Certification of conduct, training, and compliance of retail energy supplier (RES) agents/sales representatives. Filed on an occurrence basis — required when certifying that sales agents have completed required training and comply with ICC marketing and sales conduct standards. File electronically via ICC portal.
instr:
1. Ensure all RES agents complete required ICC training per 83 Ill. Adm. Code 412.170.
2. Prepare certification documenting training completion and compliance.
3. File electronically via ICC portal (requires External User Account — see ARES FAQ).
4. Retain training records for all agents.

title: Bill Format Filing
dln: Upon any new or changed bill format
desc: File bill format with ICC on an occurrence basis — required when introducing a new bill format or making material changes to an existing bill format. Ensures ARES bills comply with ICC required disclosure and format requirements. File electronically via ICC portal.
instr:
1. Before deploying any new or materially changed bill format, prepare the filing.
2. Ensure bill format complies with all ICC disclosure requirements under 83 Ill. Adm. Code 412.
3. File electronically via ICC portal prior to use.
4. Retain copy of all filed bill formats.

title: Customer Complaint and Resolution Procedures Filing
dln: Upon any new or changed procedures
desc: File customer complaint and resolution procedures with ICC on an occurrence basis — required when establishing new or materially updating existing customer complaint handling procedures. Distinct from IL-07 (responding to individual ICC-forwarded complaints). This is the filing of the procedures document itself. File electronically via ICC portal.
instr:
1. Maintain written customer complaint and resolution procedures that comply with 83 Ill. Adm. Code 412.
2. File procedures with ICC via electronic portal whenever established or materially changed.
3. Ensure procedures cover: complaint intake, investigation, response timelines, and escalation to ICC.

title: Customer Service Representative for Commission Employees
desc: Filing to designate and keep current the Customer Service Representative (CSR) contact for ICC staff — a specific point of contact at the ARES who ICC employees can reach for customer-related inquiries. File when first designating the CSR and upon any change to that designation. File electronically via ICC portal.
instr:
1. Designate a specific customer service representative as the ICC staff contact.
2. File the designation via ICC electronic portal.
3. Update within a reasonable time whenever the designated CSR changes.

title: ARES Kilowatt-Hour Report (Annual)
desc: Annual report of total kilowatt-hours delivered and sold by the ARES in Illinois during the prior calendar year. Filed with ICC by March 1. Used for RPS compliance calculations and market reporting. File using the ICC Annual Kilowatt-hour Report Form.
instr:
1. Compile total kWh delivered and sold in Illinois for the prior calendar year from settlement data.
2. Break down by customer class if required by current form.
3. Complete the ICC Annual Kilowatt-hour Report Form.
4. Include cover letter citing 83 Ill. Adm. Code 451.770.
5. File with ICC Chief Clerk's Office by March 1.

links.label: ICC ARES Webpage — KWh Report Form

title: Net Metering Report
desc: Annual net metering report filed electronically with ICC by April 1. Reports on net metering customers served during the prior year. Required for ARES with net metering customers in Illinois. If Base Power has no net metering customers, confirm whether a zero/null filing is still required.
instr:
1. Identify all net metering customers served in Illinois during the prior calendar year.
2. Compile required data per 83 Ill. Adm. Code 465.40.
3. File electronically via ICC portal by April 1.
4. If no net metering customers, confirm with ICC whether a nil report is required.

title: Municipal Aggregation Program Information
dln: 3 business days after program finalized or customer disclosures sent (whichever is earlier)
desc: File municipal aggregation program information with ICC within 3 business days of a program being determined final and made public, but no later than 3 business days after customer disclosures are sent. Required if Base Power participates in any Illinois municipal aggregation programs. Use the Municipal Aggregation Template.
instr:
1. When participating in a municipal aggregation program, track when the program is determined final and when customer disclosures are sent.
2. File program information using the ICC Municipal Aggregation Template.
3. File within 3 business days of the earlier of: program finalization or customer disclosure mailing.
4. View existing municipal aggregation list on ICC website to confirm current programs.

title: Rate Report (Annual)
desc: Annual Rate Report filed electronically with ICC by June 30. Reports rates charged to Illinois customers. Uses the ICC Rate Report Template and must include a Rate Report Certified Statement. Distinct from IL-02 (Rates Charged to Residential Customers, April 30) — this is the broader annual rate report required under 220 ILCS 5/16-115A.
instr:
1. Compile rate data for all Illinois customer classes for the reporting period.
2. Complete the ICC Rate Report Template.
3. Prepare the Rate Report Certified Statement.
4. File electronically via ICC portal by June 30.

title: Training Materials Filing (Annual)
desc: Annual filing of training materials with ICC. Documents that sales and customer service staff have received required training on Illinois customer protection rules, marketing standards, and ARES obligations. No specific due date stated — file annually. File electronically via ICC portal.
dln: Annually (specific date not prescribed — file by year-end)
instr:
1. Compile training materials used for sales agent and customer service training during the year.
2. Ensure materials cover required topics under 220 ILCS 5/16-115A(e)(vi).
3. File electronically via ICC portal.
4. Retain copies of all training materials and attendance records.

title: Consumer Education Filing
desc: Consumer education materials filed with ICC on an occurrence basis. Required when distributing consumer education materials related to electricity choice, rates, or customer rights in Illinois. File electronically via ICC portal when new consumer education materials are created or distributed.
instr:
1. When creating new consumer education materials for Illinois customers, prepare for ICC filing.
2. File electronically via ICC portal.
3. Retain copies of all consumer education materials distributed.

title: Annual Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report
desc: Annual Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report filed with ICC by June 1. Reports on the company's supplier diversity practices and spend with minority-owned, women-owned, and other diverse businesses. Use the ICC 2026 Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report Form. File electronically via ICC portal.
instr:
1. Compile supplier diversity spend data for the prior calendar year.
2. Complete the 2026 Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report Form (available on ICC ARES webpage).
3. File electronically via ICC portal by June 1.

links.label: ICC ARES Webpage — Supplier Diversity Forms

title: Customer Account Transfer Report (ICC) — One-Off
desc: File customer account transfer report with ICC within 30 days after any acquisition or divestiture of Illinois customer accounts.

title: ARES Filing Information Update — One-Off
desc: Update ARES filing information with ICC within 10 business days of any material change to company information, key personnel, or operational details.

title: Electric Quarterly Report (EQR)
desc: Quarterly electric sales report filed with FERC. Reports all wholesale electric sales transactions. Q1: Apr 30 | Q2: Jul 31 | Q3: Oct 31 | Q4: Jan 31. Filed via FERC EQR online filing system.
dln: Q1: Apr 30 | Q2: Jul 31 | Q3: Oct 31 | Q4: Jan 31

title: Notice of Change in Status (Quarterly)
desc: Quarterly change in status filing with FERC. Must report any changes in status, affiliates, or market conditions that could affect MBR authority. Filed same dates as EQR. Review all affiliates quarterly.
dln: Q1: Apr 30 | Q2: Jul 31 | Q3: Oct 31 | Q4: Jan 31

title: MBR Database Monthly Submission
desc: Monthly MBR database submission by the 15th of each month — only required if changes occurred. Includes updates to asset appendices, affiliate lists, and market conditions. Skip if no changes.
dln: 15th of each month (only if changes occurred)

title: Triennial Market Power Analysis
desc: Comprehensive market power analysis filed with FERC every 3 years (required for Category 2 MBR sellers). Analyzes horizontal and vertical market power, pivotal supplier status, and market share. Coordinate with outside regulatory counsel.

title: Asset Appendix Maintenance
desc: Maintain accurate FERC MBR asset appendices reflecting all generation, transmission, and interconnection assets. Update via eTariff filing when assets are acquired, disposed of, or materially change.

title: FERC Annual Charges
desc: Annual charges billed by FERC for MBR authority. Pay per FERC billing schedule and invoices. Track invoice receipt, due date, and payment confirmation. Late payments may result in penalties.

title: MBR Tariff Compliance
desc: Continuous compliance with FERC Market-Based Rate tariff terms and conditions. Ensure all wholesale transactions are authorized under current tariff. Monitor for tariff amendment needs triggered by business changes.

title: Record Retention — 5 Years (FERC)
desc: Maintain all FERC-related records for a minimum of 5 years as required. Includes EQR transaction data, correspondence with FERC, tariff amendments, and compliance documentation.

title: Standards of Conduct Training
dln: Annual by Dec 31; new hires within 30 days of start
desc: Annual Standards of Conduct training for all employees who interact with or have access to transmission function information. New employees must complete training within 30 days of hire. Maintain training records.

title: MBR Tariff Amendment
desc: File MBR tariff amendment with FERC 60–120 days before the desired effective date of any change to tariff terms, pricing methodology, or service offerings. Coordinate with outside regulatory counsel for complex amendments.

title: Affiliate Transaction Reporting
dln: Report in next quarterly Change in Status
desc: Report affiliate transactions in the next quarterly Change in Status filing (FE-02). Maintain an internal log of all affiliate transactions for FERC reporting purposes.

title: Peak Load Contribution (PLC) & NSPL Reporting
desc: PLC and Net Scheduled Peak Load (NSPL) reporting. Annual PLC effective June 1 for new capacity year. Daily monitoring required throughout the year. Accurate PLC is critical for determining capacity obligation.

title: Annual LSE Officer Certification
desc: Annual officer certification for Load Serving Entity (LSE) participation in PJM. Must be submitted before June 1 commencement of the new capacity year. Signed by an authorized officer of the company.

title: RPM Capacity Auction Participation
dln: Submit requirements 20–30 days before each auction
desc: Participation in Reliability Pricing Model (RPM) capacity auctions including the Base Residual Auction (BRA) and Incremental Auctions as needed. Submit capacity requirements 20–30 days before each auction. Critical for capacity procurement compliance.

title: Monthly Hourly Load Data Submission
desc: Monthly submission of hourly load data by the 10th of the following month. Required for all PJM LSEs. Data is used for load forecasting, settlement, and capacity planning.

title: Settlement Reconciliation Data Submission
dln: Last day of month + 2 months lag
desc: Monthly settlement reconciliation data submission due the last day of the month with a 2-month lag. Review and validate PJM settlement statements and submit corrections within the reconciliation window.

title: Credit Application and Maintenance (PJM)
desc: Maintain required credit and collateral with PJM per Attachment Q of the OATT. Monitor credit exposure daily. Post additional collateral when required. Failure to maintain adequate credit can result in a default declaration.

title: NITS Charges Monitoring
desc: Daily monitoring of Network Integration Transmission Service (NITS) charges billed by PJM. Conduct quarterly reconciliation against expected charges. Identify and dispute billing errors promptly within the dispute window.

title: Capacity Deficiency Compliance
desc: Year-round compliance with capacity obligations covering the June 1 – May 31 PJM capacity year. Monitor actual load vs. committed capacity positions. Procure additional capacity if deficiency is projected.

title: Load Forecast / Large Load Adjustment Requests
desc: Annual load forecast and large load adjustment requests submitted in September–October for the following planning period. Critical input for PJM capacity planning and the next Base Residual Auction.

title: Daily UCAP Obligation Tracking
desc: Daily tracking and submission of Unforced Capacity (UCAP) obligation 36 hours ahead of each operating day. Must ensure UCAP supply meets or exceeds the daily obligation. Monitor daily for compliance.

title: Regulation and Reserve Obligation Compliance
desc: Hourly compliance with PJM regulation and operating reserve obligations. Monitor actual vs. required reserve levels throughout each day. Coordinate with scheduling agent for hourly compliance.

title: Annual Credit Compliance Attestation
desc: Annual attestation of credit compliance with PJM financial assurance requirements. Certify that all collateral arrangements, credit agreements, and financial assurance mechanisms remain adequate and current.

title: FRR Plan Filing (if elected)
desc: Fixed Resource Requirement (FRR) plan filing if Base Power elects FRR as an alternative to RPM auction participation. Multi-year commitment requires careful planning. Evaluate annually whether FRR election remains appropriate vs. RPM participation.

title: New LSE Resource / Load Registration
dln: Allow 10 business days for PJM review
desc: Register new resources or load zones with PJM when adding new load zones or generation resources. Allow at least 10 business days for PJM administrative review before the target effective date.

title: IL Annual Report — Base Texas REP, LLC
dln: Last day of month before anniversary month
desc: Illinois annual report for Base Texas REP, LLC. Due the last day of the month preceding the anniversary month of original IL registration. Confirm exact anniversary date and set a calendar reminder 60 days in advance.

title: New Entity SOS Registration Tracking
desc: Track SOS registrations across all states upon new entity formation. Each new entity may trigger SOS foreign qualification, tax registration, and new recurring annual filing obligations. Update this tracker when entities are formed.

title: New Jurisdiction Compliance Checklist
desc: Comprehensive compliance checklist to complete when entering any new market. Use the New Jurisdiction Template as the starting point. Critical to complete all steps before commencing operations in any new state.
instr: Steps: (1) Register with SOS, (2) obtain EIN/state tax reg, (3) apply for PUC certification, (4) post bond/LOC, (5) file initial compliance docs, (6) register with ISO/RTO, (7) develop state-specific customer contracts, (8) add all recurring filings to this tracker.

title: ICC ORMD Annual Data Request (TC 1.01–1.05)
desc: Annual data request issued by the ICC's Office of Retail Market Development (ORMD) pursuant to Section 20-110 of the Public Utilities Act. Requires reporting of: TC 1.01 — Monthly weighted average residential rate (cents/kWh) in ComEd service area for June–May period; TC 1.02 — Same for Ameren Illinois service area; TC 1.03 — Residential municipal aggregation customer count as of May 31; TC 1.04 — Non-residential municipal aggregation customer count as of May 31; TC 1.05 — Whether company offers time-variant products to residential customers. Response must be notarized and sworn. If no residential customers served during the reporting period, submit a formal letter stating as such. Data is aggregated by ICC — no individual company data is published.
dln: June 15 each year (covers June [prior year] through May [current year])
instr:
1. Receive annual DR notice from ICC.ORMD@illinois.gov around May 18–19.
2. Complete the attached Excel spreadsheet (Average Rate Data Request) with monthly weighted average rates for ComEd (TC 1.01) and Ameren Illinois (TC 1.02) service areas.
3. Report municipal aggregation customer counts as of May 31 for TC 1.03 and TC 1.04.
4. Answer TC 1.05 regarding time-variant product offerings.
5. If Base Power had NO residential customers in the June–May period, prepare a formal letter stating this instead of the Excel form.
6. Have the response sworn and notarized using the attestation form provided in the DR.
7. Name the file: "BasePower_AnnualDR_[YEAR]" (e.g., "BasePower_AnnualDR_2026").
8. Submit to Tanya Capellan at Tanya.Capellan@illinois.gov by June 15.

completion.notes: Sent via email. This will report will be more involved next year.

title: Annual Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report
desc: Annual Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report filed with ICC by June 1. Reports on the company's supplier diversity practices and spend with minority-owned, women-owned, and other diverse businesses. Use the ICC 2026 Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report Form. File electronically via ICC portal.
instr:
1. Compile supplier diversity spend data for the prior calendar year.
2. Complete the 2026 Non-Utility Supplier Diversity Report Form (available on ICC ARES webpage).
3. File electronically via ICC portal by June 1.

links.label: ICC ARES Webpage — Supplier Diversity Forms

title: ICC ORMD Annual Data Request (TC 1.01–1.05)
desc: Annual data request issued by the ICC's Office of Retail Market Development (ORMD) pursuant to Section 20-110 of the Public Utilities Act. Requires reporting of: TC 1.01 — Monthly weighted average residential rate (cents/kWh) in ComEd service area for June–May period; TC 1.02 — Same for Ameren Illinois service area; TC 1.03 — Residential municipal aggregation customer count as of May 31; TC 1.04 — Non-residential municipal aggregation customer count as of May 31; TC 1.05 — Whether company offers time-variant products to residential customers. Response must be notarized and sworn. If no residential customers served during the reporting period, submit a formal letter stating as such. Data is aggregated by ICC — no individual company data is published.
dln: June 15 each year (covers June [prior year] through May [current year])
instr:
1. Receive annual DR notice from ICC.ORMD@illinois.gov around May 18–19.
2. Complete the attached Excel spreadsheet (Average Rate Data Request) with monthly weighted average rates for ComEd (TC 1.01) and Ameren Illinois (TC 1.02) service areas.
3. Report municipal aggregation customer counts as of May 31 for TC 1.03 and TC 1.04.
4. Answer TC 1.05 regarding time-variant product offerings.
5. If Base Power had NO residential customers in the June–May period, prepare a formal letter stating this instead of the Excel form.
6. Have the response sworn and notarized using the attestation form provided in the DR.
7. Name the file: "BasePower_AnnualDR_[YEAR]" (e.g., "BasePower_AnnualDR_2026").
8. Submit to Tanya Capellan at Tanya.Capellan@illinois.gov by June 15.

title: Annual LSE Officer Certification
desc: Annual officer certification for Load Serving Entity (LSE) participation in PJM. Must be submitted before June 1 commencement of the new capacity year. Signed by an authorized officer of the company.

title: Monthly Hourly Load Data Submission
desc: Monthly submission of hourly load data by the 10th of the following month. Required for all PJM LSEs. Data is used for load forecasting, settlement, and capacity planning.

title: MBR Database Monthly Submission
desc: Monthly MBR database submission by the 15th of each month — only required if changes occurred. Includes updates to asset appendices, affiliate lists, and market conditions. Skip if no changes.
dln: 15th of each month (only if changes occurred)
